We’re also keeping an eye on the vibratory hopper and checking the funnel scales, since double pieces can sometimes get hung up there. No issues found so far.

This was happening last week, too, right? Do you remember what the findings were?

When one of the tracking sensors is triggered for too long it will fault, if both tracking sensors are triggered it faults after a certain time. Basically we have to adjust the tracking so that it favors one side or the other and gets forced back and forth with the tracking actuation.

Visually inspecting the tote of 35g moulds, we found 60g moulds in the same tote.

No we don't have paperwork/ tracking for mold washing. One issue we noticed is at time to many EE's and Random EE's will be working in the Washroom. This causes a lot of issues like this. For day shift we are keeping the same EE's in the washroom everyday so they know whats going on

These temperatures actually indicate that the compressors aren't working as they should. You can see that only one compressor is running when the second compressor is needed. Water table temp is 34 instead of 32. We believe this is to follow the advice of Dr. Chocolate.
Shaker was at 60% at star of shift, but do not know the reason why it's higher than centerline. We reduced the shaker from 60% to 40% in order to improve the bottoms of the pieces.

Sprinkler belt was at 10% at the start of our shift, but we do not know the reason for the change from centerline. We reduced the sprinkler belt from 10% back to 9% (centerline) in order to help improve the bottoms of the pieces. The centerline for "Belt thickness HMI" needs to be changed. Centerline is listed as "47.0%", but that's not possible. With the line speed at 72, you can see that when the Sprinkler belt speed is 10%, the "Belt thickness HMI" (which is that actual speed of the sprinkler belt) shows "43%". And when the Sprinkler belt speed is set to 9%, the "Belt thickness HMI" changes to 39%. Therefore, if the centerline for the Sprinkler belt is to be 9%, the centerline for "Belt thickness HMI" should be 39%. Dakota Adair Curt Willis Justin Garner

Good morning team, at approximately 3:20 AM, the team in charge of the grinding area, working on the Graham product (Item#: 10139), noticed that almost at the end of processing the box, the bag containing the product was torn, exposing some of the material. Afterward, they inspected the box and saw that it had holes in the bottom. Since they had already processed 3/4 of the material, I will put 440 lbs of Grinded Grahams and 20 lbs of unprocessed Grahams on hold for inspection. I spoke with the team, and we agreed to inspect the box before use, and if we find anything similar, to let me know before the material is processed. It is possible that the box was mishandled by the forklift, causing the holes in the box and the inner bag.

Team,
